Hiking Day 2 — Trail Directions

Friday, May 22, 2026  •  Kumano Kodo — Nakahechi Route  •  Takahara to Chikatsuyu
Friday, May 22 — Takahara → Chikatsuyu
Transfer
Shuttle — Guest House Kiyohime → Choose Your Start Confirm choice & pickup time with owner via MyChat / WhatsApp the night before
🚔 Option A — Easier Day Request transfer to Michi-no-Eki Nakahechi on Highway 311 and begin at Gyuba-doji-guchi.
1.3 km • ~30–45 min • Difficulty 1/5 • +52m / −103m
⏱ Depart Michi-no-Eki by 15:00 • Arrive Chikatsuyu by 15:30
🏃 Option B — Full Route Request shuttle to Takahara Kiri no Sato and hike the complete trail from the Day 1 endpoint.
9.3 km • 5–6 hrs • Difficulty 3/5 • +570m elevation
📦 Luggage — Leave at Guest House Kiyohime by 9:30 AM Maruo luggage shuttle will collect 2 bags from the accommodation and deliver directly to Minshuku Chikatsuyu. Do not carry bags on the trail.
⚠ Reapply Yamabiru-fai Before Starting the Trek Reapply leech repellent to shoes, socks, and lower legs at the trailhead before setting off. The cedar and cypress forest on today’s route is active leech habitat.

Let the owner know your choice (Option A or B) when confirming pickup time. Both destinations are a short drive from Guest House Kiyohime (~10–15 min by car).

Trail Walk
Takahara Kiri no Sato → Minshuku Chikatsuyu Nakahechi Route  •  Day 2 Trail Section
⌛ Arrival Target: 17:00 at Minshuku Chikatsuyu Option A (Full): 9.3 km, ~5–6 hours. Depart Kiri no Sato no later than 10:00 AM; 09:00 recommended. Lunch at Michi-no-Eki en route.
Option B (Easier): 1.3 km, ~30–45 min from Michi-no-Eki. Transfer any time — arrive well before 17:00 with ease.
A: Depart by 10:00 AM latest B: Transfer to Michi-no-Eki ✅ Arrive by 17:00
9.3 kmDistance
5–6 hrsEst. w/ stops
+570m / −303mElevation
3 / 5Difficulty
⚔ Trail Navigation — Staying on the Main Path Trail is bilingual (English & Japanese) with numbered wooden signposts throughout (reference numbers ~18–23 on this section). No known problem junctions between Takahara and Chikatsuyu. The Highway 311 crossing near Osakamoto-oji is clearly signed — the Michi-no-Eki rest stop is directly on the far side.
  • Brown wooden signposts with white lettering mark every junction. Numbered posts appear every 500 m.
  • After the Michi-no-Eki, re-enter the trail at Gyuba-doji-guchi for the Hashiori-toge section — the entrance is marked.
  • Cobblestone staircase on the descent into Chikatsuyu — slow down, especially if wet.
  • Chikatsuyu-oji is across the bridge over the Hiki-gawa river — look for the bridge before the village.
📲 Download before leaving — offline maps:
  • Gaia GPS — load the Kumano Kodo .gpx track from walkkumano.com
  • Maps.me — .kml offline, works without signal
  • tb-kumano.jp — downloadable PDF map for this segment (save to Files app)
⚠ Terrain Note Longer and more demanding than Day 1. Sustained climbing in the first half (~570 m gain). Exposed roots and loose rocks throughout. Cobblestone staircase on the final descent into Chikatsuyu — slippery when wet. Hiking poles recommended.
■ Stamp = Official Kumano Kodo stamp station at this waypoint
  1. 1
    Kiri no Sato Takahara — Start of Trail
    Shuttle drop-off and Day 2 trailhead. This is where Day 1 ended — the trail continues forward from the rest area.
    🚽 Restroom available here — Last confirmed toilet before Jujo-oji (~3 km ahead). Use before starting.
    Photo: Tanabe City Kumano Tourism Bureau
  2. 2
    Daimon-oji  大門王子  ■ Stamp
    First oji shrine after leaving Takahara. Collect a stamp here. The trail enters deeper forest as the descent into the valley begins before the long climb ahead.
  3. 3
    Jujo-oji  十丈王子  ■ Stamp
    ~1.5 hours from Takahara. A clearing with benches — a natural rest and snack stop. Collect a stamp here.
    🚽 Restroom available here — Last confirmed toilet before the Michi-no-Eki (~5 km ahead).
  4. 4
    Uwada-jaya Teahouse Ruins  上多和茶屋跡
    The highest point of the day (~690 m), near signpost 18. Ruins of a historic pilgrim teahouse that served travelers on this route for centuries. A natural turning point — the trail begins its long descent from here. Take a moment at the top before heading down.
  5. 5
    Osakamoto-oji  逢坂元王子  ■ Stamp
    Located near a creek with a moss-lined valley and wooden bridge (between signposts 21–22). A serene, shaded spot on the descent. Collect a stamp here. The Highway 311 crossing is shortly ahead.
Trail — Michi-no-Eki Nakahechi → Minshuku Chikatsuyu Option A • Fri May 22
Option A
  1. 6
    🚔 Option A — Primary Start Point This is your starting point. Cross Highway 311 and enter the trail at Gyuba-doji-guchi directly opposite. Waypoints 2–5 are bypassed from here.
    Highway 311 Crossing → Michi-no-Eki Rest Stop
    Cross Highway 311 — clearly signed, watch for traffic. The Kumano Kodo Nakahechi Michi-no-Eki is directly on the far side. This is the recommended lunch stop for the day.
    🚽 Restroom available here — Also has a café, shop, and vending machines. Check opening hours before relying on the café.
    🆎 Lunch stop: Rest, refuel, and refill water here before the Hashiori-toge section. Re-enter the trail at Gyuba-doji-guchi on the far side of the rest stop — the trail entrance is marked.
    🐛 Leeches — Apply Yamabiru-fai Before Re-entering Trail Before re-entering the trail at Gyuba-doji-guchi, apply Yamabiru-fai leech repellent to shoes, socks, and lower legs. The Hashiori-toge forest section ahead has damp, shaded conditions where yamabiru (land leeches) are active May–October. Reapply at each rest stop.
    Wikimedia Commons — CC BY-SA 4.0
  2. 7
    Hashiori-toge Pass  箸折峠  ■ Stamp
    Re-enter the trail at Gyuba-doji-guchi and climb to Hashiori-toge. Three historic features at this pass:
    • Gyuba-doji statue (Meiji-era, ~50 cm) — depicting Emperor Kazan as a boy riding a horse and cow; a Tanabe City-designated historic site
    • 12th-century Hokyoin-to stupa — a stone funerary monument from the early Kamakura period
    • Statue of En no Gyoja — the legendary founder of Japanese mountain asceticism

    Collect a stamp here. The cobblestone staircase descent to Chikatsuyu begins from this pass.
    Wikimedia Commons — CC BY-SA 4.0
  3. 8
    Toganoki-jaya Tea House & Valley Viewpoint
    Visible on the descent from Hashiori-toge. A panoramic viewpoint opens up overlooking Chikatsuyu village in the valley below — your destination is now in sight. Take care on the cobblestone staircase, especially if wet.
    Wikimedia Commons — CC BY-SA 4.0
  4. 9
    Chikatsuyu-oji  近露王子  ■ Stamp
    Cross the bridge over the Hiki-gawa river — one of the oldest oji shrines on the Nakahechi route. Look for the bridge before reaching the village. A stamp box may be present — check on arrival. The clear mountain river marks your arrival into Chikatsuyu.
  5. 10
    Minshuku Chikatsuyu (Main Building) — End of Trail
    Check in for the night. Supper and breakfast included. Your bags will have been delivered by the Maruo luggage shuttle from Guest House Kiyohime.
    Kumano Travel Res #186433
Minshuku Chikatsuyu Nakahechi • Chikatsuyu • Fri May 22
Hotel

Check-in: 15:00–17:00. If arriving after 17:00, contact hotel in advance.

Meals: Supper & Breakfast included.

📦 Luggage Delivery 2 bags collected from Guest House Kiyohime and delivered directly to Minshuku Chikatsuyu via Maruo shuttle. Bags will be at the accommodation upon your arrival.
Minshuku Chikatsuyu — Traditional inn, Chikatsuyu, Wakayama
Kumano Travel Res #186433  |  TEL: +81-739-65-0617 (Tanabe Tourism Bureau)
